node.text: Edit what's selected to tailor your recommendations

node.content-desc: 15 Bar Ceiling Light Ideas to Brighten Any Space

The layout of Awadh Shilpgram unfolds as an elliptical spiral, gently narrowing down while spiraling inward to evoke the density and vibrancy of traditional Lucknowi 𝘣𝘢𝘻𝘢𝘢𝘳𝘴. This geometry enables smooth, seamless movement. Its architecture is conceived as an interpretative collage, where traditional elements are reimagined through contemporary construction. Arches form the inner skin of the buildings, creating a rhythmic façade that allows continuous access beneath. 𝘑𝘢𝘢𝘭𝘪 screens clad in red Agra sandstone filter daylight and air, casting patterned shadows inspired by the motifs of Lucknow’s 𝘤𝘩𝘪𝘬𝘢𝘯𝘬𝘢𝘳𝘪 embroidery. A steel structural system supports this porous envelope, allowing lightness and flexibility while retaining the tactile presence of stone. Double-heighted corridors amplify the experience of scale, recalling the grandeur of historic gateways, while cross ventilation and illuminated spaces enhance comfort within the dense bazaar environment. Craft courts, shops, food stalls and public amenities are woven into this spatial fabric, creating an immersive cultural terrain. Form, material and detail come together to create an architecture that is sensory, contextual and deeply rooted in craft, yet firmly contemporary. Photography: @fanthome | @studio_nac #lucknow #awadh #bazaar #craftsmanship #craftmarket #publicarchitecture #indianarchitecture #studioarchohm
